descriptionInUI: Runtime for the audible enemy radio net (§70 COMINT, phases C1+C2). Inert unless the mission generator emits dcsRetribution.redNet (the 'Enemy radio net (audible + DF-able)' setting is on and an enemy net station is alive). Each emitted station transmits periodic coded CW traffic (trigger.action.radioTransmission, an original synthesized morse clip) on its own fixed UHF AM frequency -- deliberately off the briefed comms plan by construction (x.500 MHz, allocated in Python against the mission's RadioRegistry), so nothing steps on friendly channels. Fixed C2 nodes (comms masts / command centers) keep a regular traffic cadence; CLANDESTINE stations -- concealed insurgent cells, IED teams, the HVT convoy, or an authored concealed comms site -- key short windows with long silence, so the hunt is catching one on the air. Tune the frequency and you HEAR the enemy net; airframes with UHF direction finding (F-4E, F-14 ARC-182 DF, F/A-18C UFC ADF, F-5E) can home on a transmission window -- the transmission is looped while a window is open, then stops, so the needle only points while they're on the air. Killing the station (an ordinary strike, recorded natively) takes its net off the air mid-mission; the plugin owns no kills and changes no gameplay model -- audio and DF geometry only. Station frequencies are logged at arm ('REDNET|: armed ...') for host-side reference; the players' brief is the COMINT kneeboard active-nets listing.

specificOptions.nameInUI: Transmission window: how long each net transmits per cycle (s)
Mean silence between a net's windows (s, jittered)
Clandestine station window: a hidden cell's transmission length (s) -- short by design, catch it or wait
Clandestine station mean silence between windows (s, jittered)
Transmitter power (W) -- RANGE only: how far away the net is receivable (DCS models the falloff). Loudness comes from the audio clip, not this.
Startup grace before the first window (s)
